name: waterfall-development
description: Enforces strict waterfall development workflow with phase gates. Use when (1) features.yml exists in project root, (2) user asks to implement/develop/build a feature, (3) user explicitly requests waterfall workflow. Creates features.yml if missing when invoked.
capabilities: Validates five phase gates before transitions
Checks requirements have descriptions and proper status
Verifies test coverage and test passing status
Blocks work that does not match current phase
Creates features.yml when missing via feature-file skill
Supports validation of specific features or all features
author: Chemiseblanc
securitySummary: Pure YAML validation tool with no network access. All 48 static findings are false positives: the scanner misinterprets markdown backticks as shell execution and phase transition arrows as cryptographic patterns. The skill only reads features.yml and validates phase gates.
valueStatement: Managing software projects without structured phases leads to missed requirements and incomplete features. This skill enforces five mandatory gates that prevent progress until each phase meets defined criteria, ensuring disciplined waterfall development.
limitations: Only supports five fixed phases (Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Complete)
Does not execute code or modify files beyond validation
No bypass mechanism for gate failures

useCases.title: Enforce governance
useCases.description: Prevent teams from advancing phases without completing required artifacts and reviews

useCases.title: Validate test coverage
useCases.description: Ensure every requirement has corresponding tests before allowing Complete phase

useCases.title: Structure new features
useCases.description: Guide teams through disciplined waterfall phases with clear entry criteria

promptTemplates.title: Basic activation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the waterfall-development skill to help me implement [feature name]

promptTemplates.title: Check status
promptTemplates.prompt: Run phase gate validation for the [feature name] feature

promptTemplates.title: Advance phase
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate that [feature name] can transition to the [Design/Implementation/Testing/Complete] phase

promptTemplates.title: Audit all
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate all phase gates for all features in this project

bestPractices: Define requirements with descriptions before attempting Design phase
Document architectural decisions before starting Implementation
Update requirement status to In-Progress when work begins
Link tests to requirements using tested-by field
antiPatterns: Advancing to Testing without test coverage
Setting requirements to Complete without verification

faq.question: What is the waterfall development model?
faq.answer: A linear, sequential approach with distinct phases: Requirements, Design, Implementation, Testing, and Complete.

faq.question: What happens if a gate fails?
faq.answer: The skill prints gate failures and stops execution. You must fix all failures before the phase transition is allowed.

faq.question: Can I skip phase gates?
faq.answer: No. There is no bypass mechanism. All gate criteria must pass before advancing to the next phase.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only validates the features.yml file. It does not execute or modify your source code.

faq.question: What is features.yml?
faq.answer: A YAML file that defines features, requirements, decisions, and test cases for tracking waterfall project progress.

faq.question: How is this different from agile?
faq.answer: Waterfall requires completing all artifacts for one phase before starting the next. Agile allows iterative work across phases simultaneously.
